etymology.description: A container and its lid -> indicates the concept of 'cover'
etymology.interpretation: 盍 (hé, "why not") depicts a container with a lid, symbolizing the action of covering. Originally, it is the graphic representation of a covered object, which later influenced its role in forming other characters with related meanings such as 蓋(蓋) (gài, "cover"). The character's modern usage extends metaphorically to form questions and imperatives that imply a proactive suggestion, similar to asking 'why not do something?'
